calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($181,172 USD / 7,533 total beneficiaries) = $24.05 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 42 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.elaboration: ["Quote: The Humane Animal Society (HAS) has been honored with the World Rabies Day Award in the 'Animal Welfare Champion' category by the Global Alliance for Rabies Control (GARC) and Boehringer Ingelheim. This accolade recognizes HAS's 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control. The award includes a cash prize and advanced tools to enhance rabies management capabilities, including the Rabies Vaccination Tracker (RVT), Vaccination and Sterilisation (VST) Tool, Rabies Case Surveillance (RCS) Tool, and Bite Case Tracker (BCT) Tool. This award was given on the One Health Day. It is an international campaign celebrating the need for a One Health approach to address shared health threats at the human-animal-environment interface.", "Highest tractability intervention found: 'Policy And Legal Advocacy'", "EA Rationale: Policy and legal advocacy can create systemic, legally-binding protections for animals, representing a highly tractable path to impact."]
details.formula: Mapping of charity's intervention types against the EA Intervention Tractability evidence base.
Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: Annual number of animals receiving vaccinations, primarily anti-rabies, in Coimbatore.
Annual number of animals receiving outpatient medical care at the facility.
Annual number of Animal Birth Control (ABC) surgeries performed to manage street animal populations.
Annual number of animals rescued from various situations in Coimbatore.
Annual number of rescued animals successfully placed in loving homes.
Annual number of animals receiving specialized surgical treatments.

significant_events.source_quote: The Humane Animal Society (HAS) has been honored with the World Rabies Day Award in the 'Animal Welfare Champion' category by the Global Alliance for Rabies Control (GARC) and Boehringer Ingelheim. This accolade recognizes HAS's 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control. The award includes a cash prize and advanced tools to enhance rabies management capabilities, including the Rabies Vaccination Tracker (RVT), Vaccination and Sterilisation (VST) Tool, Rabies Case Surveillance (RCS) Tool, and Bite Case Tracker (BCT) Tool. This award was given on the One Health Day. It is an international campaign celebrating the need for a One Health approach to address shared health threats at the human-animal-environment interface.
significant_events.summary: HAS received the World Rabies Day Award for 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control, including advanced tools.

significant_events.source_quote: From September 22nd to 28th, we conducted a week-long Anti-Rabies Vaccination Drive in Coimbatore for World Rabies Day. Our goal was to vaccinate as many animals as possible and raise public awareness about the importance of vaccinating community pets. Despite the scorching sun, our dedicated team vaccinated 927 animals.
significant_events.summary: Week-long Anti-Rabies Vaccination Drive in Coimbatore, vaccinating 927 animals and raising public awareness.

significant_events.source_quote: HAS has led the fight against animal cruelty in Coimbatore, facing challenges due to lack of legal awareness. To address this, we conducted a legal awareness session for rural police officers, supported by Coimbatore Superintendent of Police, Mr. V. Badrinarayanan (I.P.S). Dr. Mini Vasudevan explained the PCA Act and animal welfare laws. Educating law enforcement is crucial for prompt action against cruelty.
significant_events.summary: Conducted a legal awareness session for rural police officers in Coimbatore on PCA Act and animal welfare laws.

significant_events.source_quote: We conducted sessions on February 7th for primary and high school students of Yuvabharathi Public School. The interactive talks sparked active interest, and the school's fundraising activities raised substantial donations for our ABC and Rescue facility. Similarly, at Annai Velankanni Matriculation Higher Secondary School, we engaged around 550 students from grades 4 to 9 in discussions on animal welfare, inspiring thoughtful questions and compassion. We're grateful for these schools' efforts.
significant_events.summary: Conducted animal welfare sessions for primary and high school students, engaging 550+ students and raising donations.

significant_events.source_quote: The Animal Sensitization Certificate program, a collaboration between HAS and Bosch, successfully launched its second batch. This program empowers Bosch employees to aid street animals by teaching lifesaving and first aid skills. The first session provided hands-on training, while the second covered wound management, puppy care, diet, and physiotherapy.
significant_events.summary: Multiple batches of Bosch employees and other participants trained in animal first aid, rescue, wound management, and care.

significant_events.event_name: MARS Partnership for Indie Dog Adoptions
significant_events.source_quote: MARS, the company behind Pedigree and Royal Canin, partnered with our ABC and Rescue Center to promote indie dog adoptions. Their team provided essential training to our staff, focusing on adoption promotion and healthy diets for dogs. This initiative recognizes and supports the efforts of animal welfare organizations and promises to transform the lives of countless street animals.
significant_events.summary: Partnered with MARS to promote indie dog adoptions and provide staff training on adoption and healthy diets.

significant_events.source_quote: We are delighted to report the successful progress of the Bosch CSR project at the HAS Animal Birth Control Centre. The kennel renovation and kitchen revamp, sponsored by Bosch, were celebrated with esteemed guests Naved Narayan, Centre Head - Coimbatore, BGSW, and Julius Panakkal, Section Head, BGSW. Certificates were awarded to the first batch of employees completing the Animal Sensitization program, promoting compassion through training and education.
significant_events.summary: Successful Bosch CSR project for kennel renovation and kitchen revamp, with employee sensitization training.

significant_events.source_quote: We are thrilled to announce the successful completion of the Veterinary Assistant Training Program by Team HAS members at WVS India International Training Centre. Over three days, led by Ms. Elizabeth Peach from the US, the program covered anesthesia monitoring, sedation, drug administration, bandaging, and CPR. This opportunity expanded our knowledge and enhanced our veterinary care skills.
significant_events.summary: Completed a 3-day Veterinary Assistant Training Program covering anesthesia, sedation, drug admin, bandaging, and CPR.

significant_events.source_quote: The weekend concluded the month with a harmonious blend of yoga, art, and puppies through our collaboration with Pawga. The event saw overwhelming participation, and a heartwarming adoption took place. This highlights the growing awareness and support for animal welfare.
significant_events.summary: Collaboration with Pawga blending yoga, art, and puppies to promote adoptions and animal welfare awareness.

significant_events.source_quote: Our Founder and Managing Trustee, Dr. Mini Vasudevan, has been honored with the Krish Award by Sri Krishna Arts and Science College for her significant contributions to society and dedication to animal welfare. Leading our organization for 17 years, she has saved countless lives and inspired many.
significant_events.summary: Founder Dr. Mini Vasudevan honored with Krish Award for 17 years of contributions to animal welfare.

significant_events.source_quote: On World Spay Day, HAS emphasized the importance of spaying and neutering community pets to reduce street animal populations. We performed 19 surgeries, helping 16 dogs and 3 cats secure a healthier future.
significant_events.summary: Emphasized spaying/neutering importance and performed 19 surgeries (16 dogs, 3 cats) on World Spay Day.

significant_events.source_quote: We are eternally grateful to Ford for sponsoring two sets of walking wheels for our paralyzed dogs affected by road accidents. Moved by their resilience, Ford's CSR team gifted the wheels to give these dogs the mobility they deserve.
significant_events.summary: Ford sponsored two sets of walking wheels for paralyzed dogs, enhancing their mobility and quality of life.

significant_events.source_quote: Kalyan, abandoned with a broken leg, was rescued by our team and given crucial post-operative care. After a month of recovery, he was adopted by Rajeswari and her family, who welcomed him into their home with love. Munchkin, a tiny stray kitten, was rescued from a busy street by our staff. Healthy and playful, she quickly captured hearts and was adopted by Priyadarshini. Both Kalyan and Munchkin now enjoy loving, caring home.
significant_events.summary: Kalyan, a dog with a broken leg, and Munchkin, a stray kitten, were rescued, treated, and adopted into loving homes.

significant_events.source_quote: In celebration of International Pet Day, we welcomed fifteen 8th graders from Chinmaya Vidyalaya CBSE School, Vadavalli, to our ABC and Rescue Centre. The students showed remarkable empathy for street animals and generously donated treats and food for our residents. Their kindness and compassion touched us, and we hope their visit inspires them to advocate for animal welfare.
significant_events.summary: Hosted 15 8th graders at the ABC and Rescue Centre to foster empathy for street animals and inspire advocacy.

significant_events.event_name: Friendship Day Empathy Event for Children
significant_events.source_quote: To celebrate Friendship Day, long-time volunteer Senbaga Poonguzhali and HAS organized a heartwarming event for children at our ABC and Rescue Centre. Activities included a blindfolded game of "Tailing the Doggie" and an empathy-instilling storytelling session by Senbaga. Attendees received a children's book called "Saathi" by Julie Palais.
significant_events.summary: Organized an event for children at the Rescue Centre with games and storytelling to foster empathy for animals.

significant_events.source_quote: Students from Government College of Technology, Coimbatore, visited our shelter as part of their 'Pabulum' initiative, organizing a fundraiser and helping with various tasks, showing immense empathy and commitment to animal welfare. We also welcomed children from The Aalam school during their Circulus event to educate them about street animal hardships, fostering compassion and curiosity.
significant_events.summary: Students from local colleges and schools visited the shelter, volunteered, fundraised, and learned about animal welfare.

significant_events.source_quote: Our dedicated volunteers embarked on a DIY mission to create a safe, dry haven for our puppies during rainy days. Their hard work and determination transformed the space into a comfortable sanctuary, showcasing their unwavering commitment and selfless efforts.
significant_events.summary: Volunteers created a safe, dry haven for puppies during rainy days, transforming a space into a comfortable sanctuary.

significant_events.source_quote: The Humane Animal Society (HAS) received the Kerala Leadership Award for NGO Leadership on March 28th in Kochi, making it the only animal welfare organization among several recognized NGOs for their social contributions.
significant_events.summary: HAS received the Kerala Leadership Award for NGO Leadership, recognized for social contributions.

significant_events.source_quote: Welcome the new year with our EveryLife 2024 calendar, featuring captivating rescue dogs and cats and their inspiring stories. These calendars make thoughtful gifts, with all proceeds supporting HAS animals. By this month all the copies were almost sold out.
significant_events.summary: Sold EveryLife 2024 calendars featuring rescue animals, with all proceeds supporting HAS animals.

significant_events.source_quote: The Ladies Circle of Coimbatore organized a fundraiser involving their children to foster empathy for animals in need. The youngsters generously donated their savings and visited our ABC and Rescue Center to see the impact of their contributions. While saddened by tales of cruelty, the children left feeling positive and determined to help more, guided by their parents' example.
significant_events.summary: Fundraiser by Ladies Circle of Coimbatore, involving children donating savings and visiting the center to foster empathy.

significant_events.source_quote: Students from the Indian Public School, through Club Aura, raised funds for our four-legged friends by selling sustainable handmade items. They donated the proceeds to us, which we used to purchase vital items from our Amazon Wishlist. We appreciate their selfless support.
significant_events.summary: Students from Indian Public School raised funds by selling handmade items, donating proceeds for vital supplies.

significant_events.event_name: Click for a Cause Christmas Event
significant_events.source_quote: Embracing the Christmas spirit, we organized 'Click for a Cause,' where people captured moments with our furry friends against a Christmas wish tree. Participants brightened the day for our residents.
significant_events.summary: Christmas event where people photographed pets with a wish tree, brightening the day for shelter residents.

significant_events.source_quote: In celebration of World Forestry Day on March 22nd, and as part of the Green Tamil Nadu Mission, we organized a tree planting event at our ABC and Rescue Centre. The event, hosted by Forest Range Officer Pavithra, provided valuable guidance and support.
significant_events.summary: Organized a tree planting event at the ABC and Rescue Centre for World Forestry Day, supporting Green Tamil Nadu Mission.

data.original_text: We have connected more than a million pets and their families with the resources they need.

We believe pets are safest in a humane community that values and protects them, best cared for by a responsible and loving family, healthiest when seen regularly by a veterinarian, and that saving animal lives involves much more than increasing adoptions and decreasing euthanasia. That is why we have many programs and services such as counseling to help people find solutions to keep their pet, offer spay/neuter services to families in ...

One of the most effective ways to reduce euthanasia in our community is to reduce the number of homeless pets. We help keep pets and their people together by providing one-on-one counseling prior to relinquishment and offering our donor-subsidized, low-cost spay/neuter program.

By offering these much-needed services, we help save animal lives by preventing unwanted litters and work towards reducing Wichita's pet overpopulation problem. In 2023 KHS performed 8,136 spay/neuter surgeries. Of those, 1,762 were for low-income pet owners in our community.
We are committed to the prevention of abuse and neglect of animals through adoption, veterinary care, rescue & rehabilitation.

Founded in 1904, the Humane Society of New York is a nonprofit veterinary hospital and no-kill shelter dedicated to making high-quality, affordable veterinary care accessible to those who need it most.
Our fight for stronger animal welfare legislation at the city, state, and federal levels is creating a safer and more humane future, and your actions can help us continue to make a difference!
We make the world better for all animals.
Protecting over 1 billion lives annually through rescue, care, humane standards and conservation efforts.
We go where animals need us most and where few other organizations can, responding to cruelty and disaster situations around the world.
Our sanctuaries give a lifelong home to animals in need, and our rehabilitation centers have helped heal thousands of animals on their way to loving homes.

We build bridges and allies. Through grants, training, partnerships and events, we help shelters; rescue organizations; veterinarians; law enforcement officers; local, state and federal governments; community leaders; and the public change animals' lives.
We take on cruel practices. From factory farms to puppy mills, we target the most deeply rooted animal abuses affecting the greatest number of animals around the world.
Our undercover investigations, awareness campaigns and advocacy move the public, corporations and legislators to act for animals.
Caring for animals and empowering communities.
HARP operates two low-cost, high-quality veterinary medical centers, treating over 6,500 animals and performing more than 7,000 spay and neuter surgeries annually.

Beyond caring for domestic animals, HARP remains committed to indigenous species through its fully licensed Wildlife Rehabilitation Center, which treats over 4,000 sick, injured, and orphaned wildlife each year, including more than 2,300 mammals, 1,700 birds, and 50 reptiles.
The Houston Humane Society is dedicated to ending animal cruelty, abuse & the overpopulation of animals in Houston, Sugar Land, Houston Metro Area.
The main goal of our Animal Wellness Clinic is to provide affordable and accessible veterinary services to pet owners in the greater Brazoria, Ft. Bend, Harris, and surrounding counties.
Lollypop Farm, the Humane Society of Greater Rochester, provides shelter for homeless or abused animals, and programs to help pets in our community.
Our Mission is to protect and improve the lives of dogs and cats by providing shelter, care, adoption, rescue, spay/neuter programs, and community education.
We provide meaningful, humane direction through an ever-expanding lens of scientific knowledge and grassroots compassion.
We make animals our top priority when it comes to their safety, their treatment, their wellbeing and their place in our world.
1+ billion. Farm animals protected under our welfare standards.
85,000+ Animals fed, sheltered and rescued.
500,000. Animals protected at zoos and aquariums.
1.1 million. animals. have been saved each year through our work promoting plant-based eating.
2,700+ dogs. have been rescued during more than a decade of our work against the now-banned dog meat trade in South Korea.
480+ localities. have passed measures that ban the sale of puppy mill dogs in pet stores.
Since 1954, we have helped pass thousands of local and national laws protecting animals.
We have connected more than a million pets and their families with the resources they need.

And we have taught thousands of professionals how to lead effective investigations, resolve human-animal conflicts with kindness, implement high-volume, high-quality spay/neuter techniques, help street animals and pets living with their families in poverty and more.
Our save rate is now over 90% for cats and dogs, a high standard in the animal welfare community.
Thousands of dogs and cats in San Antonio and its surrounding areas are saved from euthanasia every year due to the mission and practices of the SAHS.
More than 7,000 spay and neuter surgeries annually.
The Wildlife Rehabilitation Center, which treats over 4,000 sick, injured, and orphaned wildlife each year, including more than 2,300 mammals, 1,700 birds, and 50 reptiles.
From January 1 to December 31 of 2023, KHS admitted 10,350 animals to our facility and adopted or found alternative placement for 10,037 animals with an overall Save Rate of 97%.
In 2023 KHS performed 8,136 spay/neuter surgeries. Of those, 1,762 were for low-income pet owners in our community.
In 2023, KHS transferred 2,823 animals to outside groups.
